evals.prompt: Draw a Transformer encoder-decoder architecture diagram for our machine translation paper. Encoder has 6 layers, decoder has 6 layers, source and target both English/German. Output the drawio file.
evals.expected_output: A .drawio file using the §1 template from references/drawio-templates.md. Encoder section labeled 'Encoder × 6', decoder labeled 'Decoder × 6'. Source token labels reflect English/German. K,V cross-attention is a clean horizontal line. Forward edges all satisfy source.y > target.y.

evals.prompt: I'm writing a DDPM paper and need a figure showing the forward (data → noise) and reverse (noise → data) processes as a Markov chain. Make it for IEEE single-column.
evals.expected_output: A .drawio file using the §2 Diffusion template. Two parallel chains, forward arrows blue going right, reverse arrows orange going left. x_0 in light green (data), x_T in light red (noise). Math captions below each chain.

evals.prompt: Plot this benchmark comparison: Method A: MMLU 78.2±0.5, GSM8K 75.1±0.6, HumanEval 82.0±0.4, BBH 71.5±0.7. Method B: MMLU 80.4±0.4, GSM8K 78.3±0.5, HumanEval 84.1±0.4, BBH 74.2±0.6. Ours: MMLU 83.7±0.3, GSM8K 81.9±0.4, HumanEval 87.3±0.3, BBH 78.0±0.5. Target venue is NeurIPS.
evals.expected_output: A self-contained Python script using §I-1 grouped vertical bar template. rcParams sets pdf.fonttype=42. Ours in attention-purple #9673A6, baselines in muted colors. Y-limit zoomed in. Saves both .pdf and .png. Caption discloses '±1 SD over N seeds' and the y-axis zoom.

evals.prompt: Polish this abstract for ICML. Word limit 250: 'In this paper, we propose a novel method for object detection. Our method uses transformers and we believe it is state-of-the-art. We test on COCO and get good results. The contributions are significant for the community.'
evals.expected_output: Output following polish-abstract.md format: Part 1 polished abstract in 5 parts (background → gap → method → results → impact); Part 2 word count; Part 3 sentence-to-part map; Part 4 modification log noting removed hype words ('novel', 'state-of-the-art', 'significant') and quantified results.

evals.prompt: Generate 8 candidate titles for this paper: 'We introduce DRIVE-CF, a counterfactual training method for autonomous driving safety. By imagining counterfactual crash scenarios during training, we reduce real-world collision rate by 12%.'
evals.expected_output: Output following polish-title.md format: Part 1 table of 8 candidates spanning ≥5 type categories (descriptive, result-led, question, named-method, two-part, field-positioning), each scored on Specificity/Memorability/Honesty/Searchability (1-5 each); Part 2 top-2 picks with rationale; Part 3 any deliberately skipped types.

evals.prompt: Draft a cover letter for our paper 'DRIVE-CF: Counterfactual Training for Safer Autonomous Driving' targeting Nature Communications. The work shows 12% fewer collisions on a 50-hour driving benchmark.
evals.expected_output: A 250-400 word cover letter following draft-cover-letter.md structure: 4 short paragraphs covering opening / problem-and-gap / quantified contributions / venue-fit / standard declarations. Tone confident not boastful, no hedging, active voice.

evals.prompt: We got these reviewer comments: 'R1: Section 4 lacks an ablation on the K hyperparameter. R2: The related work missed the relevant 2024 paper by Smith et al. R3: Why didn't you compare against Method-X?' Draft point-by-point responses, knowing we did add a K-ablation in revision (now Table 6) and we cite Smith et al. but missed the citation in Sec 2 (now added). Method-X uses different inputs so isn't directly comparable.
evals.expected_output: Output following respond-to-reviewers.md three-part structure (quote / response / changes) for each comment. R1 = concession (added Table 6, cite line numbers). R2 = concession (added citation in Sec 2). R3 = polite disagreement (different input modality, explain why not comparable, possibly add a note in the manuscript).

evals.prompt: Recommend the best chart type for this experimental setup: we have 5 different model architectures, each evaluated across 3 metrics (accuracy, latency, parameter count). We want to emphasize that our method achieves the best accuracy-latency trade-off.
evals.expected_output: Output following recommend-chart.md format. Primary recommendation: §I-3 Pareto front (latency vs accuracy with parameter count as bubble size or annotation), since the user explicitly wants to emphasize trade-off. Secondary: §I-1 grouped bar if they want side-by-side comparison.

evals.prompt: 帮我将以下中文草稿翻译成英文 LaTeX 学术论文段落：近年来，基于 Transformer 的大语言模型在自然语言处理领域取得了突破性进展。然而，这些模型的推理成本极高，限制了其在资源受限环境中的部署。为了解决这一问题，本文提出了一种自适应 token 剪枝方法，名为 AdaPrune。我们设计了一个可学习的剪枝模块，该模块在推理过程中动态识别并移除对最终预测贡献较小的 token。在 LLaMA-7B 和 LLaMA-13B 上的实验表明，AdaPrune 在仅牺牲不到 0.5% 精度的情况下，将推理速度提升了 1.8 倍。消融实验进一步验证了自适应阈值选择策略的有效性。
evals.expected_output: Output must follow translate-zh-to-en-latex.md two-part format. Part 1 [LaTeX]: clean English LaTeX with no bold, italic, or quotation marks; no em-dashes; LaTeX special characters (percent, underscore, ampersand) properly escaped; math mode preserved as-is. Part 2 [Translation]: Chinese back-translation for meaning verification. Present simple tense throughout for method descriptions and experimental conclusions. No itemize or enumerate environments; flowing prose only. No output beyond Parts 1 and 2.

evals.prompt: Please deeply polish this English LaTeX paragraph for NeurIPS submission. Improve academic rigor, clarity, and readability: \textit{Existing methods usually performs poorly on this task, because the data is sparse and noisy.} Our method's performance is better than the SOTA by using a novel training paradigm. The model we proposed can not only improves accuracy on clean test sets, but also reducing latency under heavy load. Previous work has show that attention mechanisms is helpful for this problem. It's worth noting that our approach don't require extensive hyperparameter searching, and it's results are stable across different random seeds. We demonstrates the effectiveness on three benchmark datasets which is widely used in the community.
evals.expected_output: Output must follow polish-en.md three-part format. Part 1 [LaTeX]: polished English with all LaTeX special chars escaped, all original citation and reference commands preserved as-is, no new formatting added. Must fix at minimum: subject-verb agreement ('methods performs' to 'methods perform'), possessives with inanimate subjects ('method's performance' to 'the performance of the method'), contractions expanded ('don't' to 'does not', 'it's' to 'its' where possessive), grammar errors ('has show' to 'has shown', 'is helpful' to 'are helpful', 'We demonstrates' to 'We demonstrate'), parallel structure restored ('improves accuracy... but reduces latency' not 'improves... reducing'), vague hype words ('novel') removed. Part 2 [Translation]: Chinese back-translation with no parenthetical English glosses. Part 3 [Modification Log]: summary of key changes made.

evals.prompt: Remove AI-generated writing patterns from this LaTeX paragraph and make it sound like a native English speaker wrote it: We delve into the intricate relationship between model capacity and generalization performance. Our extensive experiments underscore the pivotal role of data augmentation strategies, which can substantially ameliorate the robustness of the proposed framework against distribution shift. Moreover, we leverage a novel adversarial training paradigm to elucidate the underlying mechanisms that govern cross-domain transfer. The empirical results showcase that our method can foster better representation learning, thereby paving the way for future research to explore the nuanced interplay between architecture design and data efficiency.
evals.expected_output: Output must follow humanize-en.md three-part format. Part 1 [LaTeX]: rewritten text with AI-flagged vocabulary from the reference list replaced — specifically 'delve into', 'underscore', 'pivotal', 'ameliorate', 'leverage', 'elucidate', 'showcase', 'foster' must be replaced by plainer, more precise alternatives. Mechanical transitions and em-dashes removed; content flows naturally. No emphasis formatting added. Must respect intervention threshold: natural sentences left untouched. Part 2 [Translation]: Chinese back-translation. Part 3 [Modification Log]: list which AI-marked words were replaced and rationale, or output the detection-pass message if no changes were needed.

evals.prompt: Run a final consistency and logic check on this paper excerpt before submission. Only flag real errors — no style suggestions: We propose ChatDoctor-7B, a 7-billion-parameter model fine-tuned on medical dialogues. As shown in Figure 2, ChatDoctor-13B outperforms GPT-4 on the MedQA benchmark by 3.2 points. The training configuration uses a learning rate of 1e-4 with cosine decay, while Section 3.2 reports a learning rate of 5e-5 without decay. Our model achieves state-of-the-art results across all evaluation metrics, though Table 3 indicates it ranks second on the MMLU-Med subset. We use LoRA with rank r=16, and the total trainable parameters amount to 4.2M, which represents approximately 0.06% of the full model.
evals.expected_output: Output must follow check-logic.md format, reporting only fatal errors (no style suggestions). Must detect at minimum: (1) model name inconsistency — ChatDoctor-7B introduced but ChatDoctor-13B referenced in Figure 2; (2) learning rate contradiction — 1e-4 with cosine decay in training description vs 5e-5 without decay in Section 3.2; (3) claim contradiction — 'achieves SOTA across all metrics' contradicted by 'ranks second on MMLU-Med' in Table 3. Issues listed concisely in Chinese. If no fatal issues detected, output only the detection-pass message without embellishment.

evals.prompt: I am preparing a NeurIPS submission and need a broader impact statement. Paper title: 'MegaVoice: Zero-Shot Voice Cloning at Scale.' Method summary: We train a 1.2B-parameter voice cloning model on 100K hours of multilingual speech. Given 3 seconds of reference audio, MegaVoice can clone any speaker's voice and synthesize natural speech in 20+ languages. Key capabilities include emotion-preserving synthesis, accent transfer, and singing voice generation. Write an honest broader impact statement covering both positive applications and potential misuse risks. Do not exaggerate societal significance — be measured and specific.
evals.expected_output: Output must be an honest broader impact statement (new prompt: write-broader-impact). Must cover: (1) positive applications — assistive technology for speech-impaired users, multilingual education, content localization; (2) potential risks — voice impersonation fraud, non-consensual deepfake creation, erosion of voice-as-biometric trust; (3) mitigation measures — audio watermarking, consent protocols, responsible-use licensing. Tone must be measured and specific; no laundry list of generic societal benefits. Must acknowledge that voice cloning carries inherent dual-use risk that technical safeguards alone cannot fully eliminate. Avoid overclaiming significance or scope.

evals.prompt: I need to submit this English abstract in Chinese for a domestic journal. Translate it into plain Chinese text suitable for a Word document (no LaTeX, no Markdown formatting): Large language models have demonstrated remarkable capabilities in code generation tasks, yet their performance on complex algorithmic reasoning remains limited. In this work, we introduce CodeMind, a framework that combines retrieval-augmented generation with structured chain-of-thought prompting for competitive programming. CodeMind retrieves relevant algorithmic templates from a curated knowledge base and integrates them into step-by-step solution generation. Experiments on CodeForces and AtCoder benchmarks show that CodeMind improves pass@1 by 18.7% over the strongest baseline. Ablation studies confirm that both the retrieval module and the structured prompting contribute significantly to the overall improvement.
evals.expected_output: Output must be plain Chinese text suitable for direct paste into a Word document (new prompt: translate-en-to-zh-word). No Markdown formatting (no asterisks, hash marks, or backticks). No LaTeX commands in output. Full-width Chinese punctuation throughout: full-width quotation marks, Chinese commas and periods. Technical terminology must use standard Chinese academic translations where established (e.g., 'chain-of-thought prompting' as '思维链提示'). Untranslatable technical terms kept in original English where appropriate. Single block of Chinese text with no meta-commentary, no Part 1/Part 2 structure.

evals.prompt: Simulate a peer review for my paper submission. Paper: 'TokenFusion: Efficient Multi-Modal Fusion via Token-Level Gating.' Target conference: NeurIPS 2025. Brief description: We propose a light-weight gating network that decides per-token whether to fuse information from auxiliary modalities (image, audio). Unlike dense cross-attention which fuses all token pairs, TokenFusion selectively gates roughly 30% of tokens, achieving 1.5x inference speed-up. Experiments on VQA-v2, MS-COCO captioning, and How2 multi-modal translation show under 1% accuracy drop compared to full fusion. Ablations cover gating threshold sensitivity, modality weighting strategies, and the speed-accuracy trade-off curve. We also test on out-of-domain data to verify generalization. Please provide a review in Chinese, with a score from 1 to 10, strengths, weaknesses, and strategic revision advice.
evals.expected_output: Output must follow simulate-peer-review.md two-part format. Part 1 [Review Report] in Chinese with: Summary (one-sentence core claim and contribution positioning), Strengths (1-3 specific contributions — e.g., practical efficiency gain with minimal accuracy trade-off, comprehensive multi-task evaluation, out-of-domain generalization test, thorough ablation), Weaknesses (specific and actionable — e.g., whether the 30% gating rate generalizes or needs per-task tuning, whether baselines include recent efficient-attention methods, whether gating network adds measurable overhead), Rating (1-10 with one-sentence rationale referencing actual contribution level). Part 2 [Strategic Advice] in Chinese: root causes per weakness, salvageability assessment (distinguishing presentation issues from methodological flaws), action plan with specific experiments or rewrites. Full-width Chinese quotes throughout. No vague criticisms; each weakness tied to a specific setup, result, or claim. Score must reflect actual contribution — do not default to harshness.
